title: centaurea macroptilon subsp. oxylepis
intro: Centaurea macroptilon subsp. oxylepis, also known as Australian knawel, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its small yellow flowers and taproot. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat bacterial infections. The health benefits of Centaurea macroptilon subsp. oxylepis include its ability to alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin conditions, as well as its potential to support the immune system and promote wound healing. Centaurea macroptilon subsp. oxylepis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
